req.title: Hr-Admin updates onboarding-task
req.statement: An authenticated hr-admin shall be able to update a onboarding-task through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: The requirement mandates that an authenticated hr-admin can update an onboarding-task via UI form, with mandatory-field validation, audit-record creation, and HTTP 200/201 on success. Error paths cover 400 (validation), 401 (unauthenticated), and 403 (wrong role). Test design applies EP for the happy-path update, BVA for a field at its maximum-length boundary, NEG for missing mandatory fields, NEG for unauthenticated access, and DT for role-based access control. STC is applied to model the task moving through Draft -> Updated state. Together these cover all stated outcomes and error pathways while keeping each test self-contained and idempotent through fixture-based setup/teardown.

tests.name: test_hr_admin_updates_onboarding_task_valid_payload
preconditions: An existing onboarding-task record exists (created by fixture)
actions: Navigate to /onboarding-tasks/{task_id}/edit
Clear and fill all mandatory fields with valid representative values
Submit the update form
expected: HTTP 200 or 201 is returned with the entity id
The onboarding-task record reflects updated values
An audit record is written for the update operation
trace: R-WEB-026: audit record written on success
R-WEB-026: response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_hr_admin_updates_onboarding_task_valid_payload(
    hr_admin_page,        # <fixture>: authenticated Playwright page for hr-admin
    existing_task,        # <fixture>: creates+yields a task dict {id, ...}, deletes on teardown
    audit_client,         # <fixture>: thin client for audit-log assertions
    base_url,             # <fixture>: app base URL string
):
    task_id = existing_task["id"]
    page = hr_admin_page

    page.goto(f"{base_url}/onboarding-tasks/{task_id}/edit")
    page.fill("[data-testid='task-title']", "Updated Task Title EP")
    page.fill("[data-testid='task-description']", "Valid description for EP test")
    page.select_option("[data-testid='task-status']", "active")

    with page.expect_response(
        lambda r: f"/onboarding-tasks/{task_id}" in r.url
            and r.request.method in ("PUT", "PATCH", "POST")
    ) as resp_info:
        page.click("[data-testid='submit-btn']")

    response = resp_info.value
    assert response.status in (200, 201), f"Expected 200/201, got {response.status}"
    body = response.json()
    assert body.get("id") == task_id

    updated = <helper_fn>.get_task_by_id(task_id)
    assert updated["title"] == "Updated Task Title EP"

    audit_records = audit_client.get_records(entity_id=task_id, action="UPDATE")
    assert len(audit_records) >= 1, "Audit record not written"

tests.name: test_update_onboarding_task_missing_mandatory_fields_returns_400
preconditions: An existing onboarding-task record exists (created by fixture)
actions: Navigate to /onboarding-tasks/{task_id}/edit
Clear all mandatory fields leaving them empty
Attempt to submit the update form
expected: Form submission is blocked or HTTP 400 is returned
Validation error messages are displayed for each mandatory field
The onboarding-task record is not modified
trace: R-WEB-026: mandatory fields must be validated
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_update_onboarding_task_missing_mandatory_fields_returns_400(
    hr_admin_page,
    existing_task,
    audit_client,
    base_url,
):
    task_id = existing_task["id"]
    page = hr_admin_page
    original_title = existing_task["title"]

    page.goto(f"{base_url}/onboarding-tasks/{task_id}/edit")
    page.fill("[data-testid='task-title']", "")
    page.fill("[data-testid='task-description']", "")

    responses = []
    page.on("response", lambda r: responses.append(r)
        if f"/onboarding-tasks" in r.url else None)

    page.click("[data-testid='submit-btn']")

    api_responses = [r for r in responses
        if r.request.method in ("PUT", "PATCH", "POST")]

    if api_responses:
        assert api_responses[-1].status == 400, (
            f"Expected 400, got {api_responses[-1].status}")
    else:
        expect(page.locator("[data-testid='field-error']").first).to_be_visible()

    unchanged = <helper_fn>.get_task_by_id(task_id)
    assert unchanged["title"] == original_title, "Task must not be modified"

    pre_count = len(audit_client.get_records(entity_id=task_id, action="UPDATE"))
    assert pre_count == existing_task.get("_audit_count_before", 0), (
        "No new audit record should be written on validation failure")

tests.name: test_update_onboarding_task_title_at_max_length_boundary
preconditions: An existing onboarding-task record exists (created by fixture)
Maximum allowed title length is known (e.g. 255 characters)
actions: Navigate to /onboarding-tasks/{task_id}/edit
Enter a title string of exactly MAX_LENGTH characters
Fill remaining mandatory fields with valid values
Submit the update form
Repeat with MAX_LENGTH+1 characters and verify rejection
expected: Title of exactly MAX_LENGTH characters: HTTP 200/201, task updated, audit record written
Title of MAX_LENGTH+1 characters: HTTP 400 or client-side validation error, task not updated
trace: R-WEB-026: boundary value on mandatory field length
R-WEB-026: response 200/201 with entity id (at boundary)
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

MAX_TITLE_LEN = 255

def test_update_onboarding_task_title_at_max_length_boundary(
    hr_admin_page,
    existing_task,
    audit_client,
    base_url,
):
    task_id = existing_task["id"]
    page = hr_admin_page

    # --- at-boundary: MAX_LENGTH (must succeed) ---
    exact_title = "A" * MAX_TITLE_LEN
    page.goto(f"{base_url}/onboarding-tasks/{task_id}/edit")
    page.fill("[data-testid='task-title']", exact_title)
    page.fill("[data-testid='task-description']", "BVA boundary description")

    with page.expect_response(
        lambda r: f"/onboarding-tasks/{task_id}" in r.url
            and r.request.method in ("PUT", "PATCH", "POST")
    ) as resp_info:
        page.click("[data-testid='submit-btn']")

    assert resp_info.value.status in (200, 201)
    updated = <helper_fn>.get_task_by_id(task_id)
    assert updated["title"] == exact_title
    assert len(audit_client.get_records(entity_id=task_id, action="UPDATE")) >= 1

    # --- over-boundary: MAX_LENGTH+1 (must fail) ---
    over_title = "B" * (MAX_TITLE_LEN + 1)
    page.goto(f"{base_url}/onboarding-tasks/{task_id}/edit")
    page.fill("[data-testid='task-title']", over_title)
    page.fill("[data-testid='task-description']", "BVA over-boundary description")

    over_responses = []
    page.on("response", lambda r: over_responses.append(r)
        if f"/onboarding-tasks" in r.url else None)
    page.click("[data-testid='submit-btn']")

    api_resp = [r for r in over_responses
        if r.request.method in ("PUT", "PATCH", "POST")]
    if api_resp:
        assert api_resp[-1].status == 400
    else:
        expect(page.locator("[data-testid='field-error']").first).to_be_visible()

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_update_onboarding_task
preconditions: No active session / user is not authenticated
A known onboarding-task id is available
actions: Attempt to navigate to /onboarding-tasks/{task_id}/edit without authentication
Observe HTTP response or redirect
expected: HTTP 401 is returned, or user is redirected to the login page
The edit form is not rendered
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_update_onboarding_task(
    unauthenticated_page,  # <fixture>: fresh Playwright page with no session cookies
    existing_task,
    audit_client,
    base_url,
):
    task_id = existing_task["id"]
    page = unauthenticated_page

    responses = []
    page.on("response", lambda r: responses.append(r))

    page.goto(f"{base_url}/onboarding-tasks/{task_id}/edit")

    final_url = page.url
    is_redirected_to_login = "login" in final_url or "signin" in final_url

    direct_401 = any(
        r.status == 401 and f"/onboarding-tasks/{task_id}" in r.url
        for r in responses
    )

    assert is_redirected_to_login or direct_401, (
        "Expected redirect to login or 401 for unauthenticated access")

    if is_redirected_to_login:
        expect(page.locator("[data-testid='login-form']")).to_be_visible()

    audit_after = audit_client.get_records(entity_id=task_id, action="UPDATE")
    assert len(audit_after) == existing_task.get("_audit_count_before", 0), (
        "No audit record should be written for unauthenticated attempt")

tests.name: test_forbidden_role_cannot_update_onboarding_task
preconditions: User is authenticated with role:employee (not hr-admin)
actions: Authenticate as a user with role:employee
Navigate to /onboarding-tasks/{task_id}/edit
Attempt to submit a valid update payload
expected: HTTP 403 is returned, or access-denied page is shown
The onboarding-task record is not modified
No audit record is written for the update
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_forbidden_role_cannot_update_onboarding_task(
    employee_page,        # <fixture>: authenticated Playwright page for role:employee
    existing_task,
    audit_client,
    base_url,
):
    task_id = existing_task["id"]
    original_title = existing_task["title"]
    page = employee_page

    responses = []
    page.on("response", lambda r: responses.append(r))

    page.goto(f"{base_url}/onboarding-tasks/{task_id}/edit")

    is_forbidden_page = (
        page.locator("[data-testid='forbidden-message']").is_visible()
        or "403" in page.title()
        or "forbidden" in page.url.lower()
    )

    direct_403 = any(
        r.status == 403 and f"/onboarding-tasks" in r.url
        for r in responses
    )

    if not is_forbidden_page and not direct_403:
        # Form rendered; try submitting to confirm server-side enforcement
        page.fill("[data-testid='task-title']", "Forbidden Role Update")
        with page.expect_response(
            lambda r: f"/onboarding-tasks/{task_id}" in r.url
        ) as resp_info:
            page.click("[data-testid='submit-btn']")
        assert resp_info.value.status == 403, (
            f"Expected 403, got {resp_info.value.status}")

    unchanged = <helper_fn>.get_task_by_id(task_id)
    assert unchanged["title"] == original_title, "Task must not be modified by forbidden role"

    audit_after = audit_client.get_records(entity_id=task_id, action="UPDATE")
    assert len(audit_after) == existing_task.get("_audit_count_before", 0)

tests.name: test_onboarding_task_state_transitions_draft_to_active
preconditions: A task in state Draft exists (created by fixture)
actions: Navigate to /onboarding-tasks/{task_id}/edit
Change task status from Draft to Active and submit
Verify state transition and audit
Navigate back and change status from Active to Archived and submit
Verify second state transition and audit
expected: Draft -> Active: HTTP 200/201, task status is Active, audit record written
Active -> Archived: HTTP 200/201, task status is Archived, audit record written
trace: R-WEB-026: state transition coverage for status field
R-WEB-026: audit record written on each successful update
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_onboarding_task_state_transitions_draft_to_active(
    hr_admin_page,
    draft_task,           # <fixture>: creates+yields task in Draft state, deletes on teardown
    audit_client,
    base_url,
):
    task_id = draft_task["id"]
    page = hr_admin_page

    def _submit_status_change(new_status):
        page.goto(f"{base_url}/onboarding-tasks/{task_id}/edit")
        page.select_option("[data-testid='task-status']", new_status)
        with page.expect_response(
            lambda r: f"/onboarding-tasks/{task_id}" in r.url
                and r.request.method in ("PUT", "PATCH", "POST")
        ) as resp_info:
            page.click("[data-testid='submit-btn']")
        return resp_info.value

    # Draft -> Active
    resp1 = _submit_status_change("active")
    assert resp1.status in (200, 201), f"Draft->Active failed: {resp1.status}"
    task_v1 = <helper_fn>.get_task_by_id(task_id)
    assert task_v1["status"] == "active"
    audits_v1 = audit_client.get_records(entity_id=task_id, action="UPDATE")
    assert len(audits_v1) >= 1, "Audit record missing after Draft->Active"

    # Active -> Archived
    resp2 = _submit_status_change("archived")
    assert resp2.status in (200, 201), f"Active->Archived failed: {resp2.status}"
    task_v2 = <helper_fn>.get_task_by_id(task_id)
    assert task_v2["status"] == "archived"
    audits_v2 = audit_client.get_records(entity_id=task_id, action="UPDATE")
    assert len(audits_v2) >= 2, "Audit record missing after Active->Archived"
